data.title: Walmart raises pay for pharmacy technicians, offering $28/hour on average and up to $42/hour, adding 3,000 new roles.
data.description: Retail giant Walmart Inc. (NYSE: WMT) is ramping up its commitment to health care services, particularly in the area of Health & Wellness. The company announced plans to enhance the roles of 3,000 pharmacy associates nationwide, transforming their positions into new pharmacy operations team lead roles. This move includes a significant increase in hourly wages, with an average pay of $28 per hour and a possibility of earning up to $42 per hour based on location and bonus eligibility. The aim is to provide more leadership opportunities and career paths within the pharmacy sector, allowing licensed professionals to dedicate more time to direct patient care. The initiative underscores Walmart's emphasis on expanding its health care investments.

data.title: Chevron's Q4 2025 earnings: $2.8B ($1.39/share - diluted) compared to $3.2B ($1.84/share - diluted) in Q4 2024.
data.description: In the fourth quarter of 2025, Chevron Corporation (NYSE: CVX) announced earnings of $2.8 billion, equating to $1.39 per share (diluted). This figure is a decline from the $3.2 billion ($1.84 per share - diluted) earned in the same period of 2024. Notably, within the reported earnings for Q4 2025, there was a net loss totaling $128 million attributed to pension settlement costs. Foreign currency fluctuations also played a role, reducing earnings by $130 million. When adjusted, the earnings for Q4 2025 stand at $3.0 billion ($1.52 per share - diluted) in comparison to adjusted earnings of $3.6 billion in the previous year.

data.title: Banco Santander Chile reports strong financial results for 2025, with net income of Ch$1.053 billion, a 22.8% increase. ROAE at 23.5% in 12M25, NIM at 4.0%. Operating income up 10.2% YoY.
data.description: Banco Santander Chile reported solid financial results for the twelve-month period ending December 31, 2025. The bank's net income attributable to shareholders was Ch$1.053 billion ($5.59 per share and US$2.48 per ADR), a 22.8% increase from the previous year. They achieved a Return on Average Equity (ROAE) of 23.5% for 12M25, up from 20.2% in 12M24. Operating income increased 10.2% year-over-year, driven by improved net interest margin, higher fees, and financial transaction results. In the fourth quarter of 2025, net income rose 3.2% quarter-over-quarter, resulting in a 21.9% ROAE. The net interest margin improved to 4.0% in 12M25, up from 3.6% in 12M24, with net interest income and readjustments increasing by 10.9% compared to 2024. This growth was attributed to a decrease in funding costs from 4.7% to 3.8% in 12M25. Overall, the bank displayed consistent financial strength and efficiency in its operations.
